text: Meeting deadlines is a key skill in both study and work. Good time management helps you plan your tasks, breaking large projects into smaller, manageable steps. Prioritization is also essential; decide which tasks are most urgent and important, and tackle them first. Sometimes, you may need to manage the scope of your work by setting realistic goals and avoiding taking on too many extra tasks. Finally, communicate early with your team or supervisor if you foresee a problem. If you need more time, ask for it in advance rather than waiting until the last moment. These habits reduce stress and help you deliver quality work on time.
